_readme: This is an example content_brief.json showing the full schema. It is committed to the repo as a reference. Your real briefs go in briefs/[slug].json and are gitignored. This file is never used by generate.js — it is documentation only.

opportunity_model.company_stage: Series B — scaling a proven product, not 0-to-1. Role likely sits between craft and coordination.
opportunity_model.design_maturity: Emerging — JD mentions 'building the design system' and 'first design hire on the growth team', signals design is respected but not yet structured.
opportunity_model.hiring_signal: First designer embedded in a growth pod. They're not replacing anyone — they're adding design to a team that's been shipping without it.
opportunity_model.cultural_tone: Builder-first — JD uses 'ship', 'iterate', 'experiment' more than 'research' or 'process'. Velocity is the signal.
opportunity_model.recommended_positioning: Designer who ships — frames work around decisions made and outcomes reached, not process followed.

act1_who_i_am.positioning_line: I design systems that compound — the kind that make the next feature faster to ship than the last.
act1_who_i_am.philosophy_note: Good design isn't invisible. It's legible. It tells you what it does, why it's here, and what comes next.

act2_what_ive_done.intro_line: Three projects where the constraint made the work better.

works.title: Design system from scratch, 0 to shipped
works.framing_angle: Built for a growth team with no prior design infrastructure — mirrors Acme's current situation exactly.
works.decision_to_surface: Chose token-first architecture over component-first so eng could adopt incrementally without a rewrite.
works.outcome_to_surface: Reduced design-to-dev handoff time by 60% within 3 months. Team shipped 2x faster in Q3.

media.alt: Token architecture diagram showing the three-tier system
media.caption: The token architecture — global → semantic → component. This is what made incremental eng adoption possible.

works.title: Activation flow — from 22% to 41%
works.framing_angle: Growth pod work. Tight feedback loop between design, data, and product.
works.decision_to_surface: Removed the 'aha moment' gate and let users reach value before asking for commitment.
works.outcome_to_surface: Activation rate doubled in 6 weeks. Became the model for onboarding across 3 other product areas.

media.alt: Screen recording of the new activation flow
media.caption: The flow before and after — the key change was removing the paywall gate at step 2.

works.title: 0-to-1 B2B product design (NDA)
works.framing_angle: Can speak to the type of problem and the design decisions made, not the company or product name.
works.decision_to_surface: Structured the information architecture around the buyer's mental model, not the company's internal taxonomy.
works.outcome_to_surface: Product passed internal review and entered beta. Cannot share further detail.
works.nda_note: Reference as 'a B2B product design engagement in 2023'. Do not name the company or show visuals. Can describe the problem type (enterprise onboarding) and the decision made.

act3_what_ill_bring.intro_line: What the first 90 days actually look like — specific, not aspirational.

day_15.title: Map the terrain
day_15.body: Audit the current growth funnel end-to-end: flows, drop-offs, existing components, anything the team considers debt. Not to fix it yet — to understand what the team is working around and why. I'll produce a single shared document that becomes the reference for every design decision in month one.
day_15.credibility_anchor: prevco-design-system audit phase — same approach, different scale.

day_30.title: First shipped contribution
day_30.body: One real thing shipped in collaboration with the growth pod. Scoped small enough to ship clean but meaningful enough to demonstrate how I work — how I handle edge cases, how I hand off to eng, how I communicate decisions. The goal is a working pattern, not just a feature.
day_30.credibility_anchor: activation-flow-redesign first sprint — first output in week 3.

day_90.title: Foundation for the next hire
day_90.body: By day 90, there should be a design system starter, a documented component library however small, and a clear picture of where the product's visual and interaction debt lives. The measure of this isn't what I shipped — it's whether the next designer can onboard in a day.
day_90.credibility_anchor: Every design system project — this is the compounding outcome I always build toward.

cold_message.hook: I saw the growth pod designer role — building design infrastructure on a team that's been shipping without it is exactly the kind of constraint I do my best work in.
cold_message.body: I put together a page that walks through how I'd approach the first 90 days, and a couple of projects that are closest to what you're describing. Worth 5 minutes if you're still building out the shortlist.

tone_notes: Acme's JD is direct and low-jargon. Match that register — no 'leverage', no 'holistic', no 'stakeholders'. Write like a peer, not a candidate.
